title: Final Speaking Assessment
learningGoal: Demonstrate the ability to speak using past, present, and future tenses in a continuous conversation.
instructions: This is your final speaking assessment. You will have a short conversation with your teacher. You must accurately use the past, present, and future tenses in your responses. Speak clearly into the microphone.
speakingPrompt: Roleplay a conversation with your Hindi teacher on the last day of class. Greet them, state what you did yesterday, how you feel today, and your plans for next year.
scenario: It is the last day of your 3-month Hindi course. You meet your teacher, Guruji. You want to thank him and tell him about your progress and future plans.

subTasks.prompt: Greet the teacher formally and say 'Thank you for everything.'

sampleResponse.targetText: नमस्ते गुरुजी। सब कुछ के लिए धन्यवाद।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Namaste Guruji. Sab kuch ke liye dhanyawad.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Hello Guruji. Thank you for everything.

subTasks.prompt: The teacher asks what you did yesterday. Reply: 'Yesterday I studied Hindi for two hours.'

sampleResponse.targetText: कल मैंने दो घंटे हिंदी पढ़ी।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Kal maine do ghante hindi padhi.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Yesterday I studied Hindi for two hours.

subTasks.prompt: The teacher asks how you are feeling today. Reply: 'Now I am very happy because I can speak Hindi.'

sampleResponse.targetText: अब मैं बहुत खुश हूँ क्योंकि मैं हिंदी बोल सकता हूँ।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Ab main bahut khush hoon kyonki main hindi bol sakta hoon.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Now I am very happy because I can speak Hindi.

subTasks.prompt: The teacher asks about your future. Reply: 'Next year, I will come to India.'

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Next year, I will come to India.

scenarioContext.targetText: गुरुजी: नमस्ते! आज हमारी आखिरी क्लास है।
scenarioContext.transliteration: Guruji: Namaste! Aaj hamari aakhiri class hai.
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: Guruji: Hello! Today is our last class.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: नमस्ते गुरुजी। सब कुछ के लिए धन्यवाद।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Namaste Guruji. Sab kuch ke liye dhanyawad.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Hello Guruji. Thank you for everything.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: कल मैंने दो घंटे हिंदी पढ़ी।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Kal maine do ghante hindi padhi.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yesterday I studied Hindi for two hours.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: अब मैं बहुत खुश हूँ क्योंकि मैं हिंदी बोल सकता हूँ।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Ab main bahut khush hoon kyonki main hindi bol sakta hoon.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Now I am very happy because I can speak Hindi.
Next year, I will come to India.

expectedSpeakingPoints: Using 'maine padhi' for past tense studying
Using future tense for coming to India
acceptableParaphrases: Female speakers should say 'bol sakti hoon' and 'aaungi'.
Can say 'Kal maine hindi seekhi' (Yesterday I learned Hindi).

criteria.name: Content
criteria.description: Did the user answer all parts of the prompt correctly?

criteria.name: Fluency
criteria.description: Did the user speak naturally without excessive hesitation?

criteria.name: Grammar (Tenses)
criteria.description: Were the past (with 'ne'), present continuous/ability, and future tenses formed correctly?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: नमस्ते गुरुजी। सब कुछ के लिए धन्यवाद। कल मैंने दो घंटे हिंदी पढ़ी। अब मैं बहुत खुश हूँ क्योंकि मैं हिंदी बोल सकता हूँ। अगले साल, मैं भारत आऊँगा।

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic! You spoke flawlessly using all three tenses. You are ready for the final test.
feedbackRules.onFail: You had trouble switching between tenses. Make sure you use 'maine' in the past and 'ga/ge/gi' in the future.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay close attention to gender agreements in the present and future tenses.

hintText: Female speakers: remember to say 'bol sakti hoon' and 'aaungi'.
commonMistakes: Using present tense for future plans.
